topic: A samurai practicing with a nodachi.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: Front view of a samurai in a grounded stance, nodachi raised.
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: Close-up of the nodachi blade sweeping through the air as hands grip the hilt.
Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: Side-angle tracking of a full-body arc, the samurai executing a practiced nodachi swing.
parsed_generation.narration: Across eight seconds, a samurai practices with a nodachi: the sequence opens in a front view, moves to a close-up of blade motion, and closes with a side-angle full swing. The visuals stay tightly on the practitioner, emphasizing precision and discipline.

feedback:
The concept stays on topic—a samurai with a nodachi and visible blade motion—but the cadence and actions differ from the gold standard. To improve alignment, adopt a four-second cadence like [00:00-00:04] and [00:04-00:08], depict an opening draw and a decisive overhead nodachi swing, and specify camera angles (wide establishing shot, medium, close-up, and tracking) rather than only front/side views.
Failed checks: example_alignment
strengths: Clear focus on the nodachi action
Shot progression centers on the practitioner
fixes: Introduce a clear opening draw from sheath and a powerful overhead swing
Incorporate required camera angles: wide establishing shot, macro or close-up, tracking, and medium shots
